content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, and conflict_policy.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For named metric, record, score, benchmark, quota, threshold, or personal-best values, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model

Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate claims:
        - candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=records_observed_or_stated_content object="what was said or seen" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Source records what was said or seen. evidence=`Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=represents_normalized_fact object="what is considered a normalized fact" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Claim represents what is considered a normalized fact. evidence=`Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=represents_projection_layer object="compact projection of stable context" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Profile represents a compact projection of stable context. evidence=`Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=represents_operational_memory_for_future_action object="what must be done" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Task represents what must be done. evidence=`Task` = что должно быть сделано
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<4> predicate=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace object="what the system learned" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode represents what the system learned. evidence=`Episode` = чему система научилась
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<5> predicate=records_memory_update_process object="how memory was updated" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, MemoryRun records how memory was updated. evidence=`MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Existing active claims:
        - id=hot-path:claim:<0> subject=entity:<6> predicate=anchors_memory_items object="Entity anchors claims, tasks, notes, and episodes." family=anchors_memory_items policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.2 `Entity` status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Entity is an anchor for claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.
- id=hot-path:claim:<1> subject=entity:<7> predicate=must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought object="Note is not a Source, not a final Claim, and not chain-of-thought." family=must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Note must not be treated as a Source, a final Claim, or chain-of-thought.
- id=hot-path:claim:<2> subject=entity:<7> predicate=does_not_replace object=entity:<1> family=does_not_replace policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 4, why Note does not replace Claim status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Note does not replace Claim because they serve different purposes.
- id=hot-path:claim:<3> subject=entity:<5> predicate=must_not_disappear_silently object="A MemoryRun run log must not silently disappear." family=must_not_disappear_silently policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to MemoryRun run logs in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=A MemoryRun run log must not silently disappear.
- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<8> predicate=must_not_be_mixed_with object=entity:<9> family=must_not_be_mixed_with policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to truth status and retrieval_state semantics in the v3 memory data model document. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.
- id=hot-path:claim:<5> subject=entity:<4> predicate=must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought object="Episode must not store raw chain-of-thought." family=must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Episode storage rules in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode must not store raw chain-of-thought.
- id=hot-path:claim:<6> subject=entity:<3> predicate=plain_question_not_task object="A plain question must not be treated as a Task." family=plain_question_not_task policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Task classification in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, a plain question is not a Task.
- id=hot-path:claim:<7> subject=entity:<4> predicate=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace object="Episode should represent a lesson rather than a full reasoning trace." family=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Episode semantics in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode represents a lesson, not a full reasoning trace.
- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<2> predicate=excludes_disputed_facts object="disputed facts should be left in Claim or Note rather than pulled into Profile" family=excludes_disputed_facts policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Profile invariants in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, disputed facts should remain in Claim or Note rather than be pulled into Profile.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<10> predicate=has_data_model_layer object=entity:<1> family=has_data_model_layer policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Claim.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<10> predicate=has_data_model_layer object=entity:<6> family=has_data_model_layer policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Entity.
- id=hot-path:claim:<11> subject=entity:<3> predicate=request_not_commitment object="A request must not be treated as a commitment." family=request_not_commitment policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Task classification and creation in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, a request is not a commitment.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - alias_points_to_current_canonical_entity: Entity alias always points to the current canonical entity.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- anchors_memory_items: Entity serves as an anchor for memory items such as claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- can_remain_without_conversion_to_claim: Subject can remain useful long-term without conversion into a final Claim.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- can_transition_to_retrieval_state: Subject can be moved to a specified retrieval lifecycle state under stated conditions.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- candidate_claims_are_hints_only: Subject's candidate_claims_json contains hints only, not finalized claims.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- deferred_beyond_mvp: Subject is deferred beyond MVP and intended for later implementation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- does_not_replace: Subject does not replace another data model concept because they serve different roles.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- erroneous_merge_must_be_reversible_via_audit_trail: Erroneous Entity merges must be reversible through an audit trail.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- excludes_disputed_facts: Disputed facts should be excluded from Profile and left in Claim or Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- governs_semantics_of: Learned predicate for In the v3 memory data model, truth status governs whether knowledge is current, superseded, refuted, or past the end of its time window.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_allowed_state_set: Learned predicate for The common retrieval lifecycle states in the v3 memory data model are active, warm, cold, archived, and deleted.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_cross_cutting_concern: Learned predicate for The v3 memory data model has scope as a cross-cutting concern.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_data_model_layer: Learned predicate for The v3 memory data model has an evidence layer represented by Source.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- input_hash_supports_idempotency_and_retry_safety: Subject field supports idempotency and retry safety.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- intended_for: Learned predicate for scope_text is needed for explainability in the v3 memory data model.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- maturity_decreases_or_resets_when: Conditions that can decrease or reset the maturity level of a Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- maturity_increases_when: Conditions that can increase the maturity level of a Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- may_remain_explainable_with_low_top_retrieval_participation: Historical items may remain explainable while rarely participating in top retrieval.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- merge_preserves_history: Entity merge preserves history rather than deleting it.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_be_short: Subject must remain short or compact.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_be_standalone_without_source_chat: Claim must be understandable without relying on the original chat context.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_be_mixed_with: Learned predicate for truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought: Subject must not be treated as Source, final Claim, or chain-of-thought.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_change_silently: Data model invariant that Source records must not be silently modified.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_disappear_silently: Subject must not silently disappear or be removed without trace.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_duplicate_source_without_added_value: Subject must not duplicate a Source unless it adds value.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought: Subject must not store raw chain-of-thought.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- not_source_of_truth: Subject is explicitly not a source of truth.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- plain_question_not_task: A plain question must not be classified as a Task.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_explicit_facts_over_inference: Profile projection prioritizes explicit facts over inferred information.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_explicit_statement_over_inference: Claim extraction prioritizes explicit statements over inferred meaning.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- prefers_note_over_bad_claim_for_soft_meaning: Soft or uncertain meaning should be kept as a Note rather than forced into a poor Claim.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- projects_from_memory_sources: Projection layer derives from specified underlying memory sources.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- records_execution_audit_details: Subject records execution audit/debug details such as prompts, models, retrieved items, budgets, operations, and failures.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_atomic_predicate_application: Subject represents an atomic claim-level fact or relation edge in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_intermediate_semantic_fragment: Subject represents an intermediate semantic memory fragment.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace: Subject should represent a lesson rather than a full reasoning trace.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_operational_memory_for_future_action: Subject represents operational memory for a future action in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_optional_portable_experience_memory: Subject represents an optional memory type for portable experience.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_projection_layer: Subject represents a compact always-in-context projection layer.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_single_obligation: One Task should represent one clear obligation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- request_not_commitment: A request must not be treated as a commitment.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_actual_future_action: Subject should exist only when an actual future action is required.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_explicit_delete_policy: Data model invariant that deletion policy must be explicit.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_explicit_retention_policy: Data model invariant that retention policy must be explicit.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_maturity_model: Subject requires an explicit maturity model or lifecycle maturity states.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_relative_time_normalization: Relative time in a Claim must be normalized.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- responsible_for: Subject owns or is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- serves_as_audit_and_debug_layer: Subject functions as the audit trail and debug layer in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- serves_as_reconciliation_config: Subject functions as configuration used by reconciliation rather than as a memory item.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- status_must_update_not_duplicate: Status changes should update the existing item rather than create duplicates.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- stores_observed_time_separately_from_ingestion_time: Data model invariant that observed time is stored separately from ingestion time.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supports_aliases_and_merge: Entity supports aliases and merge operations.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supports_predicate_specific_reconciliation_semantics: Subject records per-predicate policy needed for reconciliation behavior.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7

responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.
responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, and conflict_policy.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For named metric, record, score, benchmark, quota, threshold, or personal-best values, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate claims:
        - candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=records_observed_or_stated_content object="what was said or seen" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Source records what was said or seen. evidence=`Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=represents_normalized_fact object="what is considered a normalized fact" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Claim represents what is considered a normalized fact. evidence=`Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=represents_projection_layer object="compact projection of stable context" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Profile represents a compact projection of stable context. evidence=`Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=represents_operational_memory_for_future_action object="what must be done" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Task represents what must be done. evidence=`Task` = что должно быть сделано
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<4> predicate=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace object="what the system learned" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode represents what the system learned. evidence=`Episode` = чему система научилась
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<5> predicate=records_memory_update_process object="how memory was updated" scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, MemoryRun records how memory was updated. evidence=`MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Existing active claims:
        - id=hot-path:claim:<0> subject=entity:<6> predicate=anchors_memory_items object="Entity anchors claims, tasks, notes, and episodes." family=anchors_memory_items policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.2 `Entity` status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Entity is an anchor for claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.
- id=hot-path:claim:<1> subject=entity:<7> predicate=must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought object="Note is not a Source, not a final Claim, and not chain-of-thought." family=must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Note must not be treated as a Source, a final Claim, or chain-of-thought.
- id=hot-path:claim:<2> subject=entity:<7> predicate=does_not_replace object=entity:<1> family=does_not_replace policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 4, why Note does not replace Claim status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Note does not replace Claim because they serve different purposes.
- id=hot-path:claim:<3> subject=entity:<5> predicate=must_not_disappear_silently object="A MemoryRun run log must not silently disappear." family=must_not_disappear_silently policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to MemoryRun run logs in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=A MemoryRun run log must not silently disappear.
- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<8> predicate=must_not_be_mixed_with object=entity:<9> family=must_not_be_mixed_with policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to truth status and retrieval_state semantics in the v3 memory data model document. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.
- id=hot-path:claim:<5> subject=entity:<4> predicate=must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought object="Episode must not store raw chain-of-thought." family=must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Episode storage rules in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode must not store raw chain-of-thought.
- id=hot-path:claim:<6> subject=entity:<3> predicate=plain_question_not_task object="A plain question must not be treated as a Task." family=plain_question_not_task policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Task classification in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, a plain question is not a Task.
- id=hot-path:claim:<7> subject=entity:<4> predicate=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace object="Episode should represent a lesson rather than a full reasoning trace." family=represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Episode semantics in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, Episode represents a lesson, not a full reasoning trace.
- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<2> predicate=excludes_disputed_facts object="disputed facts should be left in Claim or Note rather than pulled into Profile" family=excludes_disputed_facts policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Profile invariants in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, disputed facts should remain in Claim or Note rather than be pulled into Profile.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<10> predicate=has_data_model_layer object=entity:<1> family=has_data_model_layer policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Claim.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<10> predicate=has_data_model_layer object=entity:<6> family=has_data_model_layer policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Entity.
- id=hot-path:claim:<11> subject=entity:<3> predicate=request_not_commitment object="A request must not be treated as a commitment." family=request_not_commitment policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to Task classification and creation in the v3 memory data model. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the v3 memory data model, a request is not a commitment.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - alias_points_to_current_canonical_entity: Entity alias always points to the current canonical entity.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- anchors_memory_items: Entity serves as an anchor for memory items such as claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- can_remain_without_conversion_to_claim: Subject can remain useful long-term without conversion into a final Claim.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- can_transition_to_retrieval_state: Subject can be moved to a specified retrieval lifecycle state under stated conditions.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- candidate_claims_are_hints_only: Subject's candidate_claims_json contains hints only, not finalized claims.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- deferred_beyond_mvp: Subject is deferred beyond MVP and intended for later implementation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- does_not_replace: Subject does not replace another data model concept because they serve different roles.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- erroneous_merge_must_be_reversible_via_audit_trail: Erroneous Entity merges must be reversible through an audit trail.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- excludes_disputed_facts: Disputed facts should be excluded from Profile and left in Claim or Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- governs_semantics_of: Learned predicate for In the v3 memory data model, truth status governs whether knowledge is current, superseded, refuted, or past the end of its time window.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_allowed_state_set: Learned predicate for The common retrieval lifecycle states in the v3 memory data model are active, warm, cold, archived, and deleted.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_cross_cutting_concern: Learned predicate for The v3 memory data model has scope as a cross-cutting concern.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_data_model_layer: Learned predicate for The v3 memory data model has an evidence layer represented by Source.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- input_hash_supports_idempotency_and_retry_safety: Subject field supports idempotency and retry safety.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- intended_for: Learned predicate for scope_text is needed for explainability in the v3 memory data model.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- maturity_decreases_or_resets_when: Conditions that can decrease or reset the maturity level of a Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- maturity_increases_when: Conditions that can increase the maturity level of a Note.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- may_remain_explainable_with_low_top_retrieval_participation: Historical items may remain explainable while rarely participating in top retrieval.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- merge_preserves_history: Entity merge preserves history rather than deleting it.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_be_short: Subject must remain short or compact.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_be_standalone_without_source_chat: Claim must be understandable without relying on the original chat context.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_be_mixed_with: Learned predicate for truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought: Subject must not be treated as Source, final Claim, or chain-of-thought.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_change_silently: Data model invariant that Source records must not be silently modified.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_disappear_silently: Subject must not silently disappear or be removed without trace.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_duplicate_source_without_added_value: Subject must not duplicate a Source unless it adds value.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought: Subject must not store raw chain-of-thought.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- not_source_of_truth: Subject is explicitly not a source of truth.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- plain_question_not_task: A plain question must not be classified as a Task.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_explicit_facts_over_inference: Profile projection prioritizes explicit facts over inferred information.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_explicit_statement_over_inference: Claim extraction prioritizes explicit statements over inferred meaning.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- prefers_note_over_bad_claim_for_soft_meaning: Soft or uncertain meaning should be kept as a Note rather than forced into a poor Claim.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- projects_from_memory_sources: Projection layer derives from specified underlying memory sources.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- records_execution_audit_details: Subject records execution audit/debug details such as prompts, models, retrieved items, budgets, operations, and failures.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_atomic_predicate_application: Subject represents an atomic claim-level fact or relation edge in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_intermediate_semantic_fragment: Subject represents an intermediate semantic memory fragment.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace: Subject should represent a lesson rather than a full reasoning trace.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_operational_memory_for_future_action: Subject represents operational memory for a future action in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_optional_portable_experience_memory: Subject represents an optional memory type for portable experience.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_projection_layer: Subject represents a compact always-in-context projection layer.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- represents_single_obligation: One Task should represent one clear obligation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- request_not_commitment: A request must not be treated as a commitment.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_actual_future_action: Subject should exist only when an actual future action is required.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_explicit_delete_policy: Data model invariant that deletion policy must be explicit.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_explicit_retention_policy: Data model invariant that retention policy must be explicit.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_maturity_model: Subject requires an explicit maturity model or lifecycle maturity states.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_relative_time_normalization: Relative time in a Claim must be normalized.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- responsible_for: Subject owns or is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- serves_as_audit_and_debug_layer: Subject functions as the audit trail and debug layer in the data model.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- serves_as_reconciliation_config: Subject functions as configuration used by reconciliation rather than as a memory item.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- status_must_update_not_duplicate: Status changes should update the existing item rather than create duplicates.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- stores_observed_time_separately_from_ingestion_time: Data model invariant that observed time is stored separately from ingestion time.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supports_aliases_and_merge: Entity supports aliases and merge operations.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supports_predicate_specific_reconciliation_semantics: Subject records per-predicate policy needed for reconciliation behavior.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7

responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler
responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.
